title: Lemma 2.13: make the primitivity / orthogonal-summand steps explicit (reducible dichotomy)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the reducible-vertex dichotomy the final case uses two facts that are correct but should be argued explicitly:
(i) x in L(T) ∩ (L_1 ⊗ Q) implies x in L_1, using primitivity of L_1 = L(T_1) ⊕ ... ⊕ L(T_k) in L(T) (the basis V\{v} completes to V).
(ii) x = -sum_{i in A} lambda_i in L_1 with the lambda_i in orthogonal summands L(T_i)^* of L_1^* forces lambda_i in L(T_i) for each i in A.
Also the dichotomy relies on Lemma 2.11 (pi(v) generates the rank-1 lattice L_0, hence irreducible) to get xbar.ybar <= 0 with equality only if one factor is 0. These structural inputs (primitivity, orthogonality of summands, generator => irreducible) should be invoked by name where used.
